content:
# Second Brain Wiki Configuration

This file configures the skill for Evan's environment. Edit paths and
projects as your work evolves.

---

## Paths

| Path | Value | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| Wiki directory | `~/second-brain/wiki/` | Where wiki pages live |
| Sources directory | `~/second-brain/sources/` | Raw source files stored here |
| Inbox directory | `~/Desktop/Second Brain Inbox/` | Drop files here for processing |

---

## Active Projects

These are your current projects and areas of focus. The skill uses these
to find connections when ingesting new content.

- **evaluator-super-agent** — automated prototype quality assessment using persona-driven walkthroughs, 7-dimension rubric scoring, actor/evaluator separation
- **prototype-creator** — generates clickable prototypes from RFEs, tiered AC classification, Playwright automation
- **agent-eval-harness** — framework for evaluating AI skill quality, eval.yaml configs, judges, scoring pipelines
- **ai-sdlc-pipeline** — three-phase pipeline: RFE Creator (WHAT) → Prototype Creator (SHOW) → Strat Creator (HOW)
- **my-cursor-claw** — personal workspace with 20+ skills, MCP connections, notetaking, automations
- **decision-kit** — Decision Driven Development toolkit, /strategize, /decide, /journal
- **automated-usability-testing** — simulated usability testing tool, persona YAML files, evaluate-flow protocol
- **archie** — UX research agent for the RHOAIUX team
- **rhoai-prototypes** — RHOAI prototype collection for evaluation

---

## Calendar Integration

| Setting | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Calendar name | `primary` |
| Auto-ingest meetings | `true` |
| Minimum meeting duration | `15 min` |
| Require notes/transcript | `true` |
| Skip recurring without notes | `true` |

Events that qualify for auto-ingestion:
- Have attached meeting notes or a description with substance (not just "Weekly sync")
- Have a linked Google Doc (agenda, transcript, action items)
- Duration is 15+ minutes
- Are not recurring meetings that had no notes added

---

## MCP Integrations

| Integration | MCP Server | Used for |
|-------------|-----------|----------|
| Google Docs | `user-google-workspace` | Extracting doc content |
| Google Calendar | `user-google-workspace` | Meeting ingestion |
| Slack | `project-0-my-cursor-claw-slack` | Channel message ingestion |

---

## Cross-Link Priority

When multiple connections exist, prioritize these categories (from the
wiki index):

1. **Red Hat AI-SDLC** — pipeline components, RFE/prototype/strat flow
2. **Evaluation & Quality** — rubrics, heuristics, eval harness
3. **Tools & Skills** — decision kit, cursor claw, skill overlays
4. **Meetings** — meeting notes with action items and decisions
5. **Standups** — sprint progress and blockers
6. **Session Logs** — what was built and shipped

content:
1	# Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics
2	
3	Foundational UX design principles by Jakob Nielsen (1994), refined from 249 usability problems. Used as the base rubric for heuristic evaluation and automated prototype assessment.
4	
5	## Key Points
6	
7	- **1 — Visibility of System Status**: Always inform users about what's happening; feedback should be timely and predictable — builds trust
8	- **2 — Match System to Real World**: Use language and concepts familiar to users, not internal jargon; follow real-world conventions and natural mapping
9	- **3 — User Control and Freedom**: Provide clear "emergency exits" (undo/redo, cancel); prevents users from feeling trapped by accidental actions
10	- **4 — Consistency and Standards**: Don't make users guess if different words/actions mean the same thing; follow platform conventions (Jakob's Law: users spend most time on other products)
11	- **5 — Error Prevention**: Prevent problems first; two error types — slips (unconscious, inattention) and mistakes (mental model mismatch); use constraints and good defaults
12	- **6 — Recognition Rather than Recall**: Make elements/actions/options visible; don't force users to remember info across interface sections; limited short-term memory
13	- **7 — Flexibility and Efficiency**: Provide accelerators (shortcuts, gestures) for experts while keeping the interface accessible to novices; allow customization
14	- **8 — Aesthetic and Minimalist Design**: Every extra info unit competes with relevant content; not about flat design — about keeping visual elements focused on user goals
15	- **9 — Help Recover from Errors**: Error messages in plain language, precisely identify the problem, constructively suggest a solution; use visual treatments to make errors noticeable
16	- **10 — Help and Documentation**: Best if no docs needed; when required — searchable, task-focused, concise, with concrete steps
17	- Originally developed with Rolf Molich (1990); refined via factor analysis of 249 problems (1994); unchanged since then
18	- Severity ratings 1–4 commonly used when applying heuristics to specific interfaces
19	
20	## Related
21	
22	- [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) — applied these heuristics in an AI model comparison across Cursor, Claude Code, Gemini, and ChatGPT
23	- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — usability dimension is grounded in these heuristics
24	- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — automated prototype evaluation uses heuristics as scoring framework
25	- [Weekly Design Sync — July 1](weekly-design-sync-2026-07-01.md) — team adopted Nielsen's heuristics as base rubric for evaluations
26	
27	## Sources
28	
29	- https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/
30	- Ingested: 2026-07-09
31

1	# Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge
2	
3	First hands-on internship assignment: apply Nielsen Norman's 10 Usability Heuristics to Kubernetes-style interfaces across multiple AI tools and compare their analysis quality.
4	
5	## The Jagged Edge
6	
7	Research from Harvard Business School shows AI capabilities aren't uniform — models are very good at some tasks and surprisingly bad at others. The boundary between "good" and "bad" isn't obvious. This is the "jagged technological frontier."
8	
9	## What Was Tested
10	
11	- Selected Kubernetes/OpenShift UI screens at different complexity levels
12	- Crafted evaluation prompts asking each tool to assess against all 10 heuristics with severity ratings (1–4)
13	- Ran the same prompt through Cursor, Claude Code, Gemini (Flash + Pro), and ChatGPT
14	
15	## Comparison Dimensions
16	
17	- Quality of analysis — did it correctly identify real usability issues?
18	- Depth — surface-level observations vs. genuine insight?
19	- Specificity — generic advice vs. grounded in the actual interface?
20	- Structure — how well-organized and actionable?
21	- Hallucinations — did it make things up?
22	- Unique insights — did any tool catch something others missed?
23	
24	## Deliverables
25	
26	- [Jaquez - Task 1 - Nielsen & Agents](https://docs.google.com/document/d/11o9Px8wddeal3Ap7vBVtyDSw8FWaMGsuTYQwyc0hSAQ) (Google Doc)
27	- [Update — Evan Jaquez](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1AoUZtCCJf4btr68jOY3_xKbJU2wyx6XTqjGn6IIRd3w) (Google Slides)
28	
29	## Relevance to Current Work
30	
31	The comparison methodology directly informs the [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — understanding which AI tools produce research-quality heuristic evaluations vs. shallow summaries is critical for choosing the right approach to automated prototype evaluation.
32	
33	## Related
34	
35	- [Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics](nielsens-10-usability-heuristics.md) — canonical reference for all 10 heuristics with definitions and tips
36	- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — rubric-based scoring approach
37	- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — applies these learnings
38	- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — where evaluations will be used
39	- [Ansible AI Helpers Strategy — 2026-06-17](ansible-ai-helpers-strategy-2026-06-17.md) — heuristic eval skill selected as Ansible pilot
40	- [Standup — June 29](standup-2026-06-29.md) — usability comparison approach being iterated on
41	- [RH AI-First UX — June 25](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-25.md) — unified testing strategy connects to heuristic methodology
42	- [Weekly Design Sync — July 1](weekly-design-sync-2026-07-01.md) — team agreed to adopt Nielsen's heuristics as base rubric for evaluations
43	
44	## Sources
45	
46	- sources/2026-06-03-nielsen-agents-task1.html
47

1	# UX Quality Rubric
2	
3	The scoring system used by [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) to evaluate generated prototypes. Scored by a restricted `prototype-scorer` agent (no shell, no network, no MCP).
4	
5	## Dimensions (0–2 each, 8 total)
6	
7	| Dimension | What It Measures |
8	|---|---|
9	| **Completeness** | Does the prototype cover the RFE's user stories and acceptance criteria? |
10	| **Usability** | Is the interaction pattern clear and free of obvious friction? (Nielsen's 10 heuristics) |
11	| **Feasibility** | Can this be built with the target design system (PatternFly 6)? |
12	| **Fidelity Match** | Does the prototype match the requested fidelity level — not over or under-engineered? |
13	
14	## Pass Threshold
15	
16	6+ total with no zeros. Prototypes scoring above get `prototype-creator-rubric-pass`. Below get `prototype-creator-needs-attention`.
17	
18	## Scoring Agent
19	
20	One invocation per dimension ensures scores are deterministic and auditable. The scorer agent is restricted — no shell, no network, no MCP — to prevent contamination.
21	
22	## Connection to Evaluator Super Agent
23	
24	The [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) will extend this rubric with additional dimensions from simulated usability and desirability testing. See also [Nielsen's Heuristics](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) for the research that informs the usability dimension.
25	
26	## Related
27	
28	- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — the pipeline that uses this rubric
29	- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — extends this scoring
30	- [Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics](nielsens-10-usability-heuristics.md) — canonical reference for the 10 heuristics underlying the usability dimension
31	- [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) — informs the usability dimension via AI model comparison
32	- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — similar pattern of rubric-based automated scoring
33	- [Weekly Design Sync — July 1](weekly-design-sync-2026-07-01.md) — scoring dimensions flagged for revisit at next meeting
34	
35	## Sources
36	
37	- sources/2026-06-09-prototype-creator-overview.html
38	- andybraren/prototype-creator config/ux-rubric.yaml
39

1	# Evaluator Super Agent
2	
3	Evan's primary assignment: build an evaluation agent that automates prototype quality assessment within the [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) pipeline.
4	
5	**Status: Shipped.** The evaluator is a 3-phase skill pipeline on branch `evan/prototype-evaluate` at [andybraren/prototype-creator](https://github.com/andybraren/prototype-creator). It has evaluated 18+ STRATs against Jira acceptance criteria with Playwright persona walkthroughs.
6	
7	## What It Does (current)
8	
9	| Capability | Source | Status |
10	|---|---|---|
11	| AC evaluation | Custom Playwright persona journeys against Jira criteria | Shipped |
12	| Usability scoring | 7-dimension rubric with persona overlays (Zack Bodnar's framework) | Shipped |
13	| Consistency checking | Beau Morley's consistency checker (source + visual mode) | Shipped |
14	| Navigation reachability | Playwright verifies pages are clickable from sidebar | Shipped |
15	| Human review | Review tab in HTML report with override buttons + CSV writeback | Shipped |
16	| Report hosting | GitLab Pages (eval-reports-7ebc0e.pages.redhat.com) | Shipped |
17	| Google Sheet sync | Automated pass/fail + usability metrics to tracking sheet | Shipped |
18	| Feedback loop | Eval results → prototype-refine → re-eval (max 3 iterations) | In progress |
19	
20	## Architecture (confirmed June 16 with Andy)
21	
22	Evolved from "single monolithic super agent" to a **phased pipeline with overlays**:
23	
24	```
25	Phase 1: prototype-evaluate (extract ACs, RFE, Outcome, personas, journeys)
26	    ↓ writes extract-state.json
27	Phase 2: evaluate-journey (Playwright walkthroughs, consistency check)
28	    ↓ writes journey-log.json, screenshots, CSV
29	Phase 3: evaluate-usability (7-dimension persona scoring)
30	    ↓ updates journey-log.json
31	Phase 4: render-report.js + sync-sheet.js (HTML, CSV, Google Sheet)
32	```
33	
34	**Key design decisions:**
35	1. **Outcomes over Strategic Goals** as grounding criteria (Outcomes are consistently written)
36	2. **One Playwright pass** — shared screenshots across usability, consistency, and AC checks
37	3. **98% generic + 2% product overlay** (`config/product-overlay.yaml`) — portable to other products
38	4. **Hint mechanism** for dead-end navigation (failure logged but journey continues)
39	5. **Human-in-the-loop** — Review tab for designer input before feedback loop acts
40	
41	## Research References
42	
43	- **Avenir-UX** — autonomous UX evaluation agent using SEQ/SUS + persona framework
44	- **UXCascade** — multi-agent persona simulation with severity-rated issues (S1–S4)
45	- GitLab Notes REST API for MR comment posting
46	- Danger bot framework for CI-integrated review automation
47	
48	## How It Connects
49	
50	```
51	Jira STRAT (Outcome → RFE → STRAT)
52	    → prototype-create (generates code in workspace)
53	    → prototype-evaluate (this agent — 3 phases)
54	        ├── Phase 1: Extract ACs, personas, journeys from Jira
55	        ├── Phase 2: Playwright journeys + consistency check
56	        ├── Phase 3: Usability dimension scoring
57	        └── Phase 4: Report + Sheet sync
58	    → Human reviews report (override verdicts, add notes)
59	    → prototype-refine (acts on suggestions + human annotations)
60	    → Re-evaluate (loop until PASS or max iterations)
61	    → prototype-submit (MR to GitLab)
62	```
63	
64	## Key Design Inputs from Demo (May 14)
65	
66	From the [Prototype Creator Demo](prototype-creator-demo-may14.md):
67	- Consistency checkers should only evaluate what's NEW (CSS class="new" tagging)
68	- Even auto-mode should produce decision-kit pages for post-review
69	- Evaluator needs outcome-level awareness, not just feature-level
70	- Preview URL is the evaluation entry point (load the MR URL, assess in browser context)
71	- Token efficiency matters — scope evaluation to changed areas only
72	
73	## Related
74	
75	- [Outcome Tiger Team](outcome-tiger-team.md) — proposes outcome-level awareness for evaluation, directly extending this agent's scope
76	- [Prototype Creator Demo — May 14](prototype-creator-demo-may14.md) — walkthrough with design insights for this agent
77	- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — the pipeline this plugs into
78	- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — existing scoring to extend
79	- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — pattern for automated eval loops
80	- [Decision Kit](decision-kit.md) — using /strategize to plan this agent
81	- [Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics](nielsens-10-usability-heuristics.md) — the canonical heuristics that inform usability dimension scoring
82	- [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) — informs methodology choice via AI model comparison
83	- [My Cursor Claw](my-cursor-claw.md) — the workspace where this will be built
84	- [Ansible AI Helpers Strategy — 2026-06-17](ansible-ai-helpers-strategy-2026-06-17.md) — heuristic eval pilot connects to automated evaluation vision
85	
86	## Updates from June 16, 2026 Review
87	
88	Decisions made with Andy Braren (see [Andy/Evan Eval Review — June 16](andy-evan-eval-review-2026-06-16.md)):
89	
90	- **Architecture confirmed:** Centralized super evaluator, not fragmented skills. One Playwright pass, shared screenshots.
91	- **Grounding confirmed:** Outcomes as primary criteria, Strategic Goals excluded for now.
92	- **Overlay approach adopted:** 98% generic + 2% product-specific layer. See [GitLab skill-overlays best practice](https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/global-engineering/wg-agentic-sdlc/-/blob/main/best-practices/skill-overlays.md).
93	- **Hint mechanism shipped:** Simulated UX researcher provides next URL on failure, allowing evaluation to continue while logging failure impact.
94	- **Screenshot consolidation:** Sub-agents share one set of Playwright screenshots instead of each capturing independently.
95	
96	- [RH AI-First UX — 2026-06-18](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-18.md) — Evan demoed automated usability testing + PatternFly consistency checker to full team
97	- [Andy/Evan Meeting — June 22](andy-evan-2026-06-22.md) — eval iteration history, flagging system, Prototype Creator integration strategy
98	- [AI-First Sync — June 25](ai-first-sync-2026-06-25.md) — Evan's full evaluator presentation to the team, readiness check discussion
99	- [RH AI-First UX — June 25](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-25.md) — testing fragmentation concerns, unified testing proposal
100	- [Standup — June 29](standup-2026-06-29.md) — 2 automated usability testing agents in development
101	- [Skill Overlays](skill-overlays.md) — context engineering technique relevant to team-specific overlays
102	- [Prompt Engineering Best Practices](prompt-engineering-best-practices.md) — sequential prompting and format-controlled output underpin the 3-phase evaluation pipeline
103	- [Andy/Evan Meeting — June 29](andy-evan-2026-06-29.md) — findings tab, auto-fix vs report-only modes, X-ray vs blind evaluation, centralized dashboard
104	- [Midpoint Check-in — June 30](midpoint-checkin-2026-06-30.md) — Zack: "pioneering work" combining research + AI + SDLC; report must be immediately actionable for designers
105	- [Weekly Design Sync — July 1](weekly-design-sync-2026-07-01.md) — Evan to demo evaluator next week; Nielsen's heuristics confirmed as base rubric
106	
107	## Sources
108	
109	- [Andy/Evan Eval Review — June 16](andy-evan-eval-review-2026-06-16.md)
110	- Andy/Evan meeting notes — 2026-06-08
111	- sources/2026-06-09-prototype-creator-overview.html
112	- /strategize session — 2026-06-09 (in progress)

content:
1	# Prototype Creator
2	
3	Phase 2 ("the SHOW") of Red Hat's [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md). Takes RFE user stories and produces rapid prototypes that make the proposed experience tangible before engineering commits.
4	
5	## What It Does
6	
7	Given an RFE from the [RFE Creator](rfe-creator.md) pipeline or Jira directly, this pipeline:
8	
9	1. **Create** — Generates a prototype from RFE user stories at a specified fidelity level
10	2. **Review** — Scores against a 4-dimension [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md)
11	3. **Refine** — Iterates based on review feedback and human direction
12	4. **Test** — Runs simulated usability and desirability testing
13	5. **Submit** — Publishes the finished prototype to a target system
14	
15	## Two Modes
16	
17	- `--mode=auto` (CI/batch) — AI makes all design decisions autonomously. Good for low-fidelity at scale.
18	- `--mode=decide` (human-in-the-loop) — Stops at each design decision point and produces a [Decision Kit](decision-kit.md) artifact with options, previews, tradeoffs. Nothing advances without human judgment.
19	
20	## Fidelity Levels
21	
22	- **Low** — Wireframe, placeholder boxes, static (~2–3 min)
23	- **Medium** — PatternFly 6 components, key flows (~5–10 min)
24	- **High** — Production-ready, all flows + edge cases (~15–30 min)
25	
26	## Standalone vs. Workspace Mode
27	
28	- **Standalone** — Self-contained HTML files using PatternFly CDN
29	- **Workspace** (`--workspace`) — Targets an existing codebase, analyzes tech stack, generates code that fits the project (React, Angular, etc.)
30	
31	## Key Constraints
32	
33	- Internal GitLab instance does not support unique URLs per merge request via GitLab Pages
34	- Prototypes must be served via localhost during evaluation
35	- Code Rabbit not available on internal GitLab — custom commenting needed
36	
37	## Evaluator Super Agent (Evan's assignment)
38	
39	Building an evaluator that combines usability testing, desirability testing, consistency checking (Bo Morley's tool), and design feasibility scoring. Uses Jira as context source. Posts automated feedback on merge requests.
40	
41	See: [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md)
42	
43	## Related
44	
45	- [Outcome Tiger Team](outcome-tiger-team.md) — Workstream 2 proposes E2E prototype validation against outcome goals
46	- [Prototype Creator Demo — May 14](prototype-creator-demo-may14.md) — full walkthrough of both workflows with team discussion
47	- [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md) — the three-phase pipeline
48	- [Decision Kit](decision-kit.md) — integrated for human-in-the-loop decisions
49	- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — for evaluating skill quality
50	- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — scoring system
51	- [Session Log — 2026-06-15](session-log-2026-06-15.md) — crafted Slack response about prototype-evaluate for #wg-agent-eval-harness
52	- [Ansible AI Helpers Strategy — 2026-06-17](ansible-ai-helpers-strategy-2026-06-17.md) — next candidate for Ansible integration after heuristic eval pilot
53	- [RH AI-First UX — 2026-06-18](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-18.md) — GitLab pages routing fix enables multi-prototype hosting; Evan demoed evaluate work
54	- [Andy/Evan Meeting — June 22](andy-evan-2026-06-22.md) — cost strategy for Prototype Creator invocation in eval loop
55	- [Zack/Evan Meeting — June 24](zack-evan-2026-06-24.md) — Jira epic structure, skill architecture, context window limits
56	- [AI-First Sync — June 25](ai-first-sync-2026-06-25.md) — 3.6 branch strategy, centralized reporting goals
57	- [Andy/Evan Meeting — June 29](andy-evan-2026-06-29.md) — sub-skill structure, RF→Strat entity linking, token optimization
58	- [Midpoint Check-in — June 30](midpoint-checkin-2026-06-30.md) — where prototype creator fits in ADLC still TBD, report as product
59	
60	## Sources
61	
62	- sources/2026-06-09-prototype-creator-overview.html
63	- [andybraren/prototype-creator](https://github.com/andybraren/prototype-creator) (GitHub)

1	# RH AI-First UX -- June 25, 2026
2	
3	Weekly AI-first UX team meeting covering Outcome Creator, testing strategy, and infrastructure alignment.
4	
5	## Key Discussion Points
6	
7	- **Outcome Creator JTBD integration:** Yahav tested Jobs to be Done data in Outcome Creator. Successfully linked specific jobs to outcomes. Bug: skill writes artifact files to project root instead of workspace.
8	- **Personnel update:** Beau on medical leave for several months. Team adjusting Kiwi-related work.
9	- **Quality Engineering transition:** QE team transitioning under Ron's management. Yahav got handover doc with context on UXD-within-QE initiative. Kamesh facilitating intro.
10	- **Agentic CI and skill orchestration:** Need for a higher-level orchestrator that determines which skills are executed within the RHAI pipeline. Zack shared "Agentic CI" docs (library for packaging skills into agentic packages).
11	- **Infrastructure standardization:** Lack of clarity in mapping between different infrastructure concepts (blue box, implementation zone). UIE aims to standardize.
12	- **Models of service outcomes:** Andy used Outcome Creator for models of service/inference outcomes. Planning to present at PM-wide meeting next Wednesday. PMs appreciated JTBD data integration.
13	- **Testing fragmentation:** Zack flagged proliferation of varied prototype/usability testing tools across the org (Yonyi's team, prototype evaluator, heuristic analysis skills). Team needs to establish canonical version or unified standard.
14	- **Unified testing proposal:** Yahav assigned to draft formal proposal for review next week defining dimensions and requirements for standard usability testing approach.
15	- **Artifact storage pattern:** Team agreed to establish a common design pattern for storing project artifacts consistently across all skills.
16	
17	## Decisions
18	
19	- Common artifact storage pattern to be established across skills
20	- Unified usability testing strategy proposal to be drafted
21	- Journeys and milestones reside within outcome level in Jira architecture
22	
23	## Related
24	
25	- [Outcome Tiger Team](outcome-tiger-team.md) -- outcome creator progress and JTBD integration
26	- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) -- part of the testing fragmentation discussion
27	- [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md) -- agentic CI and skill orchestration
28	- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) -- artifact storage pattern
29	- [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) -- unified testing strategy context
30	
31	## Sources

text:
Here's what your wiki knows about **usability testing**:

---

## Direct Matches

### [Evaluator Super Agent](~/second-brain/wiki/evaluator-super-agent.md) — your primary work
Your main assignment: an automated prototype quality assessment agent that runs simulated usability testing via Playwright persona walkthroughs. **Status: Shipped** on branch `evan/prototype-evaluate` at `andybraren/prototype-creator`. Key capabilities:
- **7-dimension usability scoring** with persona overlays (Zack Bodnar's framework)
- **Playwright-based persona journeys** evaluated against Jira acceptance criteria
- **3-phase pipeline**: extract ACs/personas → Playwright walkthroughs + consistency check → usability dimension scoring → HTML report + Google Sheet sync
- Research references include **Avenir-UX** (autonomous UX eval agent using SEQ/SUS) and **UXCascade** (multi-agent persona simulation with severity-rated issues S1–S4)
- A feedback loop (eval → prototype-refine → re-eval, max 3 iterations) is in progress

### [Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics](~/second-brain/wiki/nielsens-10-usability-heuristics.md)
Foundational reference — Jakob Nielsen's 1994 heuristics (visibility, match to real world, user control, consistency, error prevention, recognition over recall, flexibility, minimalist design, error recovery, help/docs). Your team adopted these as the **base rubric** for all evaluations (confirmed at the July 1 design sync).

### [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](~/second-brain/wiki/nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md)
Your first internship assignment: applied heuristic evaluation across Cursor, Claude Code, Gemini, and ChatGPT to Kubernetes/OpenShift UI screens. Compared analysis quality, depth, specificity, hallucinations, and unique insights. This directly informed the Evaluator Super Agent's methodology.

### [UX Quality Rubric](~/second-brain/wiki/ux-quality-rubric.md)
The 4-dimension scoring system (Completeness, Usability, Feasibility, Fidelity Match) used by Prototype Creator. The **Usability** dimension is grounded in Nielsen's heuristics. Pass threshold: 6+ total with no zeros.
